protocol_gap_note: Official upstream SDK repositories currently advertise A2A Protocol Specification v0.3.0, so live interop runs must record any shape or capability deltas relative to Talos' standards-first A2A v1 adapter.

targets.rationale: This is the lowest-friction official upstream server path called out in the current A2A Python SDK documentation and is the best first validation target for Talos.
notes: Run the sample server in one terminal, then point the Talos Python and TypeScript live smokes at the resulting base URL.
The official A2A Python SDK README says the SDK implements A2A Protocol Specification v0.3.0.

targets.rationale: This is the current official JavaScript SDK line. The bundled sample agent is the next best follow-up target after the Python sample because it exercises a second upstream codebase and transport stack with a concrete runnable server.
notes: The official A2A JS SDK README says the SDK implements A2A Protocol Specification v0.3.0.
The bundled sample agent listens on http://localhost:41241 and advertises supportsAuthenticatedExtendedCard=false, so extended discovery should be skipped rather than treated as a failure.

targets.rationale: This is the official JVM server line and provides a third independent upstream implementation once the Python path is clean.
notes: The a2a-java repository includes a dedicated TCK and reference transports.
The current hello-world server needs a reactor install of sibling snapshot modules before the example itself will boot from a fresh clone.
The live sample advertises a v0.3-style Agent Card but uses a hybrid transport shape: root-path canonical JSON-RPC plus REST-style message and task endpoints instead of Talos /rpc.

targets.rationale: This is the official compatibility suite for spec compliance and should be run after the basic live smokes succeed against a chosen upstream target.
notes: Use the TCK after the Talos live smokes have already identified the expected auth and discovery path.
The TCK README says it validates A2A Protocol v0.3.0 compliance.
